frameworks.name: multivariate pattern analysis / neural decoding
frameworks.one_line: decode WM contents from distributed delay-period fMRI activity patterns

frameworks.name: cross-modality decoding
frameworks.one_line: train on one feature, test on another to probe shared abstract code

frameworks.name: hierarchical visual representation
frameworks.one_line: low-level sensory codes vs higher-level abstract feature codes

core_question: What is the representational format of contents held in visual working memory across the visual hierarchy?
key_assumptions: WM contents are decodable from delay-period spatial activity patterns
orientation and motion direction share a candidate abstract feature axis
neurons in early visual cortex carry feature-specific tuning while higher areas may carry abstract codes
manipulations: stimulus type maintained during delay (gabor orientation vs random-dot-kinematogram motion direction)
12s delay period of a delayed estimation WM task
key_findings: WM contents could be decoded from distributed delay-period activity across many maps
in some maps, a decoder trained on gabor orientation successfully decoded dot-motion direction (and vice versa)
cross-modality decoding succeeds despite mismatched low-level sensory features
interpretations: the format of the WM representation is not the low-level visual feature itself
some cortical maps maintain a more abstract feature code shared across modalities
